summary: R4 fixes are implemented. The postwar arrangement guard no longer depends on incoming payload.localPolityId. It now inspects current district control plus M3 administrative marker state for the district, rejecting when an existing non-direct or non-controller administrative marker indicates an already-applied postwar arrangement. The systems repro variants using localPolityId equal to the prior victor marker now reject before stale rights can survive or new overlapping obligations can be added.

tests_run.result: R4 red pass before implementation: new localPolityId bypass regression failed because direct-control/tribute-only by victor 3 with localPolityId 1 was accepted.
Formatted the touched R4 implementation and test files.

decisions: R4 conflict rule: hasExistingPostwarGovernanceArrangement now receives world and districtId only; it no longer receives or trusts payload.localPolityId.
The guard rejects when the current district has an M3 administrative marker with a non-direct control mode, or an administrative marker held by a polity other than the current district controller.
No replacement semantics were added. Clearing or retiring previous suzerain edges, obligations, and enfeoffments remains out of the minimal M3-safe boundary.

Regression coverage now includes victor 1 restore-vassal-ruler followed by victor 3 direct-control and tribute-only with localPolityId changed to 1. Both assert rejection, unchanged state hash, unchanged controller, no victor 3 administrative marker, and no victor 3 obligations.

Previous R2/R3 protections remain covered: same-victor duplicates reject, direct-after-vassal rejects, different-victor with original local polity rejects, and restore-vassal-ruler requires concrete ruler/policy fields.
risks: R3 public protocol surface remains changed from the original M3 implementation and still needs systems_architect review for long-term command/query naming and compatibility.

The guard remains a minimal M3 prototype rule over existing M3 administrative marker state; a future replacement command should define explicit retirement events and rights-clearing semantics before allowing same-district replacement.
Projection coefficients remain prototype constants for M3 validation and are not final balance.
requested_action: Review the R4 payload-independent district marker guard and the localPolityId bypass regression coverage, then route to QA if systems review accepts.
